codeReview.summary: The implementation is complete and correct. All 15 traceability tests pass, the code builds successfully, and no clippy issues were found. The feature provides bidirectional links: Goal→Criteria→Tests→Results→Evidence as specified.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The feature val-traceability-store has workerSessionIds: [] and status: 'pending' in features.json despite having a complete implementation in commit 0dcc2b9. This indicates the feature was not processed through the normal worker session pipeline with handoffs and transcript files.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: features.json shows workerSessionIds: [], status: 'pending' but the traceability.rs file exists with 2155 lines of implementation and all 15 tests pass

sharedStateObservations.observation: The feature exports renamed types (TraceAcceptanceCriteria, TraceTestCase, TraceTestResult) to avoid conflicts with same-named types from test_planning and evidence modules. This naming convention is appropriate and documented in a comment in lib.rs.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: crates/swell-validation/src/lib.rs:107 - '// Note: TestCase and TestResult from traceability conflict with same names from test_planning and evidence'

summary: Review PASS - The val-traceability-store feature is correctly implemented with bidirectional traceability links (Goal→Criteria→Tests→Results→Evidence). The implementation includes a TraceabilityStore trait with InMemoryTraceabilityStore (testing) and SqliteTraceabilityStore (production), full chain navigation (get_full_chain, get_reverse_chain), and comprehensive test coverage (15 tests). All tests pass, code builds, and no clippy issues exist. Note: The feature lacks worker session tracking (workerSessionIds: []) suggesting it bypassed the normal worker pipeline, but the implementation quality is satisfactory.
